description: Apply the cash flow additivity principle — the value of combined cash flow streams equals the sum of their present values, underpinning the no-arbitrage condition in asset pricing

fields.name: cash_flow_streams
fields.label: Array of cash flow streams; each stream is an array of {period, amount}

fields.name: discount_rate
fields.label: Common discount rate (or term structure) used to evaluate each stream

core_principle.statement: If two portfolios produce the same cash flows at the same times, they must have the same value today — otherwise arbitrage exists
core_principle.mathematical: PV(A + B) = PV(A) + PV(B) when evaluated at same time with same discount function

no_arbitrage_condition.rule: Price(Portfolio_1) == Price(Portfolio_2) if cash flows are identical in amount and timing
no_arbitrage_condition.violation_implies_arbitrage: Any price difference is a risk-free profit opportunity; arbitrage forces prices back into line

applications.name: Implied forward rates
applications.mechanism: Long-dated bond = rolling sequence of shorter bonds

applications.name: Forward exchange rates
applications.mechanism: Covered interest parity — two currencies, two rates

applications.name: Option pricing
applications.mechanism: Replicating portfolio of bond + underlying yields option payoff

applications.name: Bond stripping
applications.mechanism: Coupon bond = portfolio of zero-coupon bonds

preconditions.same_timing: Cash flows must occur at identical dates to be directly additive
preconditions.same_discount_function: All streams must be discounted using a consistent term structure
preconditions.frictionless_markets: No transaction costs, no bid-ask spread, unlimited borrowing at risk-free rate

violation_detection.triangulation: Combine three instruments to detect cross-market mispricing

validation.non_empty_streams: cash_flow_streams must contain at least two streams
validation.aligned_periods: Cash flow dates must be representable as comparable periods

evaluate_streams.description: Compute PV of each stream and the combined PV

detect_arbitrage.description: Compare PV of two economically equivalent streams

errors.message: At least one cash flow stream must be provided
Cash flow dates cannot be aligned to a common schedule

anti_patterns.rule: Do not add cash flows that occur at different dates without first discounting them
anti_patterns.why: Additivity applies only at a common evaluation point; adding nominal amounts across time conflates dollars of different values

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore transaction costs and bid-ask spreads in real-world arbitrage checks
anti_patterns.why: Academic no-arbitrage is frictionless; a 5bp 'arbitrage' that costs 10bp to execute is not an arbitrage

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply a single discount rate across currencies
anti_patterns.why: Each currency has its own risk-free curve; combining currencies requires forward FX (covered interest parity)

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume additivity holds across non-linear payoffs (options) without replication
anti_patterns.why: Option payoffs are convex; value the replicating portfolio, not the nominal payoff sum

goals.description: Apply the cash flow additivity principle — the value of combined cash flow streams equals the sum of their present values, underpinning the no-arbitrage condition in asset pricing

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details

worked_example_stripping.equivalence: Sum of PVs of 5, 5, 5, 100 discounted at 5% = 100
worked_example_stripping.interpretation: Coupon bond = portfolio of zero-coupon bonds

worked_example_arbitrage.scenario: Bond A prices at 102, synthetic replication via strips prices at 101
worked_example_arbitrage.action: Buy synthetic (101), sell bond (102), lock in 1.00 risk-free
